title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · Graph Lens: Gita Commitgraph Bloom Midx
summary: A busy monorepo accumulates packs, stale worktrees, slow reachability walks, background jobs, and backup gaps while developers and CI operate concurrently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita commitgraph bloom midx increment in measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Commitgraph Bloom Midx
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G57 is a theory in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-commitgraph-bloom-midx — Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning
git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack
# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need

steps.title: Build Gita Commitgraph Bloom Midx evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-commitgraph-bloom-midx-vd6exd" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Commitgraph Bloom Midx into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-commitgraph-bloom-midx-vd6exd
# scenario-key: trace-vd6exd probe-dxe6dv boundary-6exdvd owner-vd6exdx fault-vd6exdz
# before-ref: main=a11cevd candidate=b00b1xd
# hypothesis: measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning

# expected: commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected
# observation: history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion
# changed-case: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs
# after-ref: main=a11cevd candidate=c0ffeed
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair
# transfer-limit: benchmark and schedule real maintenance per filesystem, Git version, repository size, and uptime need
